message:
readme: complete the visual tour — Backends + Bundle + Playground + Audit screenshots (issue #5)

Iter 58 + 60 added Stats / App Store / Connect screenshots. The other
4 routes (Backends, Bundle, Playground, Audit) didn't have hero images
yet. Issue #5 closes the gap.

Wrapped in <details> so the README's main flow stays scannable —
operators who want to see the screens click the disclosures. Each
image:
  - 1280x611-ish viewport (1440 chrome window, 900 height, body
    selector trims chrome chrome)
  - Captioned with the route's load-bearing UX moment (refusal-color
    highlighting on Audit; witness comparator framing on Bundle;
    decision trace on Playground; cache-pressure + federation graph
    on Backends)
  - Wrapped in <a href> to ruvnet.github.io/RuLake/ so each screenshot
    doubles as a click-through to the live demo
  - Shows the '● LIVE · rulake-mcp.ruv.io' pill in the top-right —
    the screenshots themselves prove the production wire works

Total assets/ now: console-hero.png (Stats) + console-appstore.png +
console-connect.png + console-browse.png + console-bundle.png +
console-playground.png + console-audit.png = 7 of 7 routes.

files.patch:
@@ -821,10 +821,44 @@ Three modes: - **Demo** — animated mock data, no dependencies. - **WASM-local** — `rulake-wasm` running in your browser; verify bundles, compute witnesses, search via Web Worker. Zero server required. -- **Live MCP** — point at any running `rulake-mcp` (or `mcp-rvdna`) and the same UI drives the live server. +- **Live MCP** — point at any running `rulake-mcp` (or `mcp-rvdna`, or `mcp-ruqu`) and the same UI drives the live server. The 7th route is an **App Store** that lists every shipped substrate with install commands for the Rust crate, MCP companion, and (where applicable) npm package. +#### The other four routes + +
+📊 Backends — collections + cache pressure + federation topology + + + +Drill into any collection to inspect its witness in the Bundle viewer. +
+ +
+🧾 Bundle — witness comparator (publisher vs recomputed-in-browser) + + + +Two columns of the comparator: **Publisher** is what the lake says, **Recomputed** is what your browser hashed independently. They must match — that's the contract. +
+ +
+🛝 Playground — submit `rulake_query` intents and inspect the decision trace + + + +Pick a target, set k + risk, click Send. The browser independently recomputes the witness on the response and shows MATCH only when it agrees with the publisher's signature. +
+ +
+📓 Audit — JSONL ledger with refusal codes + + + +Tail of every tool call with timestamp, principal, target, latency, outcome code. Refusals (e.g. `WITNESS_MISMATCH_REFUSED`) get the refused color so an operator can spot trust violations at a glance. +
+ ### End-to-end smoke contracts Three scripts cover the wire from different angles. Each runs in seconds and exits non-zero on the first failure — drop any one of them into CI to catch regressions:
